name: Apple Juice Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Par boil and cut sweet potatoes into pieces.
2. Cook onions in 1 tablespoon butter or shortening in a Dutch oven until tender-crisp; set aside.
3. Brown the roast in remaining butter or shortening in the Dutch oven over medium heat for 15 to 20 minutes, or until browned on both sides. Pierce the entire surface of the meat with a fork.
4. Combine apple juice, catsup, salt, pepper, thyme, mustard, and basil; add to the meat.
5. Top the meat with reserved cooked onions; cover and cook slowly for 2 hours and 30 minutes, or until almost tender.
6. Brush sweet potatoes with lemon juice for bright color; add to the meat.
7. Continue cooking, covered, for 30 to 40 minutes, or until meat and potatoes are tender.
8. Place meat and potatoes on a warm platter. Sprinkle potatoes with chopped parsley or garnish with apple rings and parsley, if desired.
9. Serve gravy over sliced meat.
NOTE: After the apple juice mixture is added to the browned meat, it may be marinated in the refrigerator for 3 to 4 hours before serving time. Turn the meat several times. If using a cast iron Dutch oven, transfer to a glass dish.
GRAVY: Skim excess fat from cooking liquid; add water if needed to make 1 and 1/2 cups. Mix 1/2 cup water and 2 tablespoons unbleached flour; stir gradually into the cooking liquid. Heat to boiling; cook, stirring for 3 to 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper, if desired. Gravy may be served in a large apple that has been scooped out, if desired.
